claim: M4 Max MacBook Pro delivers 15-25% faster token generation than M2 Max across model sizes from 1B to 72B parameters.
actionable: If considering an M2 to M4 Max upgrade for local LLM inference, expect 20% improvement in tokens per second across all model sizes tested.
source_title: M4 MAX MacBook Pro BENCHMARKED: Deepseek v3 vs Qwen, Phi-4 and Llama on Ollama

claim: Token throughput drops below 10 tokens per second with 72B parameter models on M4 Max, marking the practical usability ceiling for interactive local development.
actionable: For interactive workflows on M4 Max with 128GB RAM, use models up to 32B parameters (20+ tokens/sec) and treat 72B+ as batch-only tasks.
source_title: M4 MAX MacBook Pro BENCHMARKED: Deepseek v3 vs Qwen, Phi-4 and Llama on Ollama

claim: Benchmarking local models requires verifiable outputs (pass/fail evaluation) rather than subjective assessment; executable code with deterministic results is ideal.
actionable: Design benchmarks around executable code (Python functions) where success/failure is determined by runtime output rather than human judgment.
source_title: M4 MAX MacBook Pro BENCHMARKED: Deepseek v3 vs Qwen, Phi-4 and Llama on Ollama

claim: Falcon 3 (10B parameters) provides exceptional performance (67 tokens/sec on M4) and accuracy parity with larger models, making it underrated for local inference.
actionable: Evaluate Falcon 3 10B as primary model for local inference if latency sensitivity exists; it provides 67 tokens/sec with strong code/math accuracy.
source_title: M4 MAX MacBook Pro BENCHMARKED: Deepseek v3 vs Qwen, Phi-4 and Llama on Ollama

claim: CLI task completion tasks (parsing natural language to shell commands) show significant accuracy degradation even in state-of-the-art models, failing 70%+ of test cases.
actionable: Do not rely on models for direct CLI generation without verification; always require human review or execution testing of generated shell commands.
source_title: M4 MAX MacBook Pro BENCHMARKED: Deepseek v3 vs Qwen, Phi-4 and Llama on Ollama

claim: Simple arithmetic operations in Python are reliably solved by models as small as 3B parameters; you do not need larger models for basic math.
actionable: For simple math arithmetic tasks, deploy 3B models (Llama 3.2 1B-3B) to maximize inference speed without sacrificing correctness.
source_title: M4 MAX MacBook Pro BENCHMARKED: Deepseek v3 vs Qwen, Phi-4 and Llama on Ollama
